title: senecio spathulifolius subsp. arvernensis
intro: Senecio spathulifolius subsp. arvernensis is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Senecio genus, commonly referred to as a type of ragwort plant. Native to the Dordogne region in southwestern France, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Senecio spathulifolius subsp. arvernensis are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or salves, although it is essential to exercise caution when using it, as excessive consumption can be toxic due to the presence of pyrrolizidine alkaloids.
